dhp320:2: enduring an arrow shot from a bow–
dhp320:4: for the mass of people have no principles.
dhp321:1: The tamed is the one they take into assemblies.
dhp321:2: The tamed is the one the king mounts.
dhp321:3: The tamed who endures a false accusation
dhp321:4: is, among human beings, the best.
dhp322:2: tamed thoroughbreds, tamed horses from Sindh.
dhp322:4: But even more excellent are those self-tamed.
dhp323:1: For not by these mounts could you go
dhp324:2: deep in rut, is hard to control.
dhp324:3: Bound, he won’t eat a morsel:
dhp324:4: the tusker misses the elephant wood.
dhp325:3: like a stout hog, fattened on fodder:
dhp325:4: a dullard enters the womb over & over again.
dhp326:2: however it pleased, wherever it wanted, by whatever way that it liked.
dhp326:3: Today I will hold it aptly in check–
dhp326:4: as one wielding a goad, an elephant in rut.
dhp327:3: Lift yourself up from the hard-going way,
dhp327:4: like a tusker sunk in the mud.
dhp328:1: If you gain a mature companion–
dhp329:1: If you don’t gain a mature companion–
dhp329:2: a fellow traveler, right-living, enlightened– go alone
dhp329:3: like a king renouncing his kingdom,
dhp329:4: like the elephant in the Matanga wilds, his herd.
dhp330:2: There’s no companionship with a fool.
dhp330:3: Go alone, doing no evil, at peace,
dhp330:4: like the elephant in the Matanga wilds.
dhp331:1:
A blessing:
friends when the need arises.
dhp331:2:
A blessing:
contentment with whatever there is.
dhp331:3: Merit at the ending of life is a blessing.
dhp331:4:
A blessing:
the abandoning of all suffering & stress.
dhp332:1:
A blessing in the world:
reverence to your mother.
dhp332:2:
A blessing:
reverence to your father as well.
dhp332:3:
A blessing in the world:
reverence to a contemplative.
dhp332:4:
A blessing:
reverence for a brahman, too.
dhp333:1: A blessing into old age is virtue.
dhp333:4: The non-doing of evil things is a blessing.
